description: A length or portion of time; a distinct interval with beginning and end. 'Mosha' names bounded time—the span between markers, the duration of a state or process.
sound_symbolism: The word spans and holds. 'Mo' grounds the beginning, duration starting. Then 'sha' /ʃa/ extends through, the period continuing.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo mosha' (the period). More general than 'kaero' (phase) which implies part of a larger cycle.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Periods marked life—the planting period, the harvest period, times with distinct character and requirements.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: All periods end—recognizing the bounded nature of difficult times brings patience, of good times brings appreciation.
